title: World Cup injury tracker: Latest news on Davies, Mitoma and other key potential absentees - The Athletic
original_text:
World Cup injury tracker: Latest news on Davies, Mitoma and other key potential absentees
Some of soccer's biggest stars have had their seasons disrupted or ended early by injuries Alex Grimm/Getty Images Clive Rose/Getty Images
The Athletic has live coverage of the latest 2026 World Cup news.
The World Cup is a chance to see some of the planet’s best players compete on the biggest stage of them all.
However, like any other competition, it is not immune to the injury bug. Several key names have already been ruled out with long-term issues, others face a race against time to be ready for the trip to North America, while some are battling back to fitness before the end of their club’s domestic seasons.
There will be 48 countries at the World Cup for the first time, meaning that the pool of players hoping for a call-up this summer has drastically increased.
Here, The Athletic takes you through the key active injuries of all 48 nations in our tracker, which will be updated throughout the build-up to the tournament.
Group A
Mexico
Luis Malagon (GK) — Club America goalkeeper ruptured his Achilles in March and will miss the World Cup. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Julian Araujo (RB) — Out with a hamstring injury since March, bringing his loan spell at Celtic to an early end, and was not included in the World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: July 2025
Rodrigo Huescas (RB) — Copenhagen confirmed that Huescas underwent surgery for an anterior cruciate ligament injury (ACL) in October and would be out for the season. He was not included in the World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: September 2025
Marcel Ruiz (CM) — Toluca midfielder suffered an ACL tear in March but returned in April after opting to not undergo surgery. Was not included in the World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: February 2026
South Africa
N/A
South Korea
Park Yong-woo (DM) — Tore ACL while playing for Abu Dhabi-based club Al Ain on September 25 and not included in World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: September 2025
Czech Republic
Vitezslav Jaros (GK) — Liverpool goalkeeper had his loan spell at Ajax ended early by what the Merseyside club described as a “serious knee injury” and was not included in World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: September 2025
Matej Rynes (CM) — Slavia Prague midfielder suffered a muscle injury in April and did not make the World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Group B
Canada
Alphonso Davies (LB) — Hamstring injury sustained on May 6 while playing for Bayern Munich will keep the 25-year-old out for “several weeks”, and is a doubt for opening group game. Last national team appearance: March 2025
Ralph Priso (DM) — Sustained a left hamstring strain playing for Canada on March 31, the Vancouver Whitecaps confirmed the defender would be out for eight to 10 weeks. Was not included in the World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Ali Ahmed (LW) — Included in the World Cup squad despite being forced off during Norwich City’s final game of the Championship season. Last national team appearance: April 2026.
Moise Bombito (CB) — Set to be taken out of Canada’s squad after being seen limping following his first minutes since breaking his leg in October. Last national team appearance: June 2026
Bosnia & Herzegovina
Adrian Leon Barisic (CB) — An adductor injury has kept the Braga centre-back out since early March, and he was not included in the World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: October 2025
Qatar
N/A
Switzerland
N/A
Group C
Brazil
Vanderson (RB) — The full-back sustained a hamstring injury playing for Monaco on March 6 and subsequently underwent surgery. He was not included in Brazil’s World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: June 2025
Eder Militao (CB) — Real Madrid defender sustained a left hamstring injury in late April and missed the end of the season. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Rodrygo (RW) — Tore his ACL and meniscus in his right knee playing for Real Madrid in early March, ruled out of the World Cup. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Estevao (RW) — Not included in Brazil’s World Cup squad due to a hamstring injury suffered on April 18. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Estevao is in his first season at ChelseaGlyn Kirk/AFP via Getty Images
Vitor Roque (ST) — Palmeiras striker underwent surgery for a left ankle injury in left April and was not included in the World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Neymar (FW) — Sidelined with a calf injury sustained at the end of May which is due to keep him out for “two to three weeks”.
Wesley (RB) — Replaced by Ederson in the Brazil squad on June 7 due to a muscular injury to his left thigh. Last national team appearance: June 2026
Morocco
Nayef Aguerd (CB) — Has not featured since undergoing surgery in early March to treat a persistent groin issue, but was named in World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: January 2026
Hamza Igamane (ST) — Striker tore his ACL while playing for Morocco in the Africa Cup of Nations final on January 26 and will miss the World Cup. Last national team appearance: January 2025
Abdessamad Ezzalzouli (LW) — Forced off with a leg injury during his side’s final pre-World Cup warm up match, with head coach Mohamed ⁠Ouahbi saying the team were waiting to learn of the severity. Last national team appearance: June 2026
Noussair Mazraoui (RB) — Forced off with a shoulder injury during Morocco’s final pre-tournament friendly. Ouahbi explained his team were waiting to discover the extent of the injury. Last national team appearance: June 2026
Haiti
N/A
Scotland
Billy Gilmour (MF) — The Napoli midfielder was named in Scotland’s initial 26-man squad but was ruled out of the World Cup after sustaining a right knee injury in a pre-tournament friendly match against Curacao. Last national team appearance: May 2026.
Group D
United States
Jonathan Klinsmann (GK) — Sustained a broken neck playing for Cesena on April 18 and underwent successful surgery the following week. Last national team call-up: March 2026
Cameron Carter-Vickers (CB) — Sustained an Achilles injury in October, with then-Celtic manager Brendan Rodgers saying that he would be out for between “three and five months”. Last national team appearance: March 2025
Chris Richards (CB) — Landed awkwardly after an aerial duel while playing for Crystal Palace against Brentford on May 17. Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner said he has torn two ligaments in his ankle, and he did not feature in the USMNT’s final two warm up games. Last national team appearance: March 2026
John Tolkin (LB) — Picked up an “inner ligament injury to his knee” playing for German club Holstein Kiel on April 10, out for multiple weeks and not named in the World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: November 2025
James Sands (CM) — Underwent ankle surgery in March, ruling him out for the rest of the season. Last national team appearance: October 2025
Johnny Cardoso (CM) — Expected to miss the World Cup after undergoing ankle surgery. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Tanner Tessmann (CM) — Sidelined with a muscle strain until the end of the season and, though his World Cup availability was not expected to be affected, he was not named in the squad. Last national team appearance: April 2026
Quinn Sullivan (CM) — Suffered an ACL tear playing for the Philadelphia Union in September. Last national team appearance: June 2025
Patrick Agyemang (ST) — Sustained a serious Achilles injury while in action for Derby County on April 6, and will not play at the World Cup. Last national team appearance: April 2026
Agyemang had been in good form for DerbyJosep Lago/AFP via Getty Images
Paraguay
Julio Enciso (FW) — Set to be assessed after being taken off on a stretcher in tears in final pre-World Cup friendly. Paraguay head coach Gustavo Alfaro said he hoped he could return for Paraguay’s first group game. Last national team appearance: June 2026
Australia
Callum Elder (LB) — Had knee surgery in early March, did not play for the remainder of the season and was not included in the World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Lewis Miller (RB) — The Blackburn Rovers defender ruptured his Achilles on February 14, is set for around nine months out and will miss the World Cup. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Nicholas D’Agostino (ST) — Sustained a knee injury on February 28 and not included in World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: October 2025
Noah Botic (ST) — Austria Wien forward broke his ankle in training in late November (also tore the syndesmotic and medial ligaments), expected to miss six to nine months. Last international call-up: September 2025
Turkey
N/A
Group E
Germany
Marc-Andre Ter Stegen (GK) — Underwent hamstring surgery on February 7, just over two weeks after joining Girona on loan from Barcelona, and was not included in the World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: June 2025
Serge Gnabry (ST) — Sustained an adductor tear in his right thigh, confirmed on April 22 that he would miss the World Cup. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Lennart Karl (MF) — The 18-year-old was forced to withdraw from the World Cup squad just two weeks before his side’s opening game after suffering a muscle bundle tear in his left thigh during training. Last national team appearance: May 2026
Curacao
N/A
Ivory Coast
N/A
Ecuador
Patrik Mercado (CM) — Tore ACL playing for Independiente del Valle on March 22. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Leonardo Campana (ST) — Ruled himself out of World Cup with social media statement after unspecified injury. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Group F
Netherlands
Matthijs de Ligt (CB) — Sidelined since November 30 with a back injury that subsequently required surgery and misses the tournament. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Jurrien Timber (RB) — Forced to pull out of squad having been deemed to have “not recovered sufficiently” from the groin injury that sidelined him at the end of Arsenal’s 2025-26 season. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Jerdy Schouten (DM) — Sustained ACL tear in early April, will miss the rest of the season and World Cup. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Xavi Simons (AM) — Suffered an ACL injury playing for Tottenham Hotspur on April 25. Last national team appearance: March 20
Simons’ will miss the World Cup(Michael Steele/Getty Images
Japan
Koki Machida (CB) — Hoffenheim defender sustained an ACL tear in late August. Last national team appearance: June 2025
Kaoru Mitoma (LW) — Suffered a hamstring injury playing for Brighton on May 9 and has not been included in Japan’s World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Takumi Minamino (LW) — Tore ACL in Monaco’s game against Auxerre on December 22. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Yuito Suzuki (ST) — Sustained a fractured collarbone playing for Freiburg against Wolfsburg on May 3 but included in Japan’s World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Wataru Endo (CM) — Made just eight league appearances for Liverpool in 2025-26 and struggled with an ankle injury, played 45 minutes in pre-tournament friendly against Iceland. Last national team appearance: May 2026
Sweden
Emil Krafth (RB) — Newcastle defender underwent knee surgery in February, ruling him out for the season and he was not named in Sweden’s World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Gustav Lundgren (RW) — Suffered an Achilles injury playing for Swedish team GAIS on April 6 and not named in World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Dejan Kulusevski (RW) — Has not been included in the squad after not playing all season due to an ongoing knee injury that has required surgery. Last national team appearance: November 2024
Emil Holm (RB) — Replaced in squad by Herman Johansson on May 30 after sustaining a muscular injury. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Tunisia
Dylan Bronn (CB) — Servette defender picked up a knee issue in February. Returned to the bench for the final two games of the season and named in World Cup squad, but yet to feature in competitive fixture for club or country since initial injury. Last national team appearance: January 2026
Hannibal Mejbri (CM) — Appeared in discomfort as he was withdrawn on 78 minutes in Tunisia’s friendly against Austria. Was an unused substitute in subsequent match against Belgium.
Group G
Belgium
Thibaut Courtois (GK) — Sustained a thigh injury against Manchester City on March 17, out for four to six weeks. Appeared in pre-World Cup friendlies Last national team appearance: June 2026
Amadou Onana (CM) — Sustained knee injury playing for Aston Villa on April 30. Appeared in pre-World Cup friendlies. Last national team appearance: June 2026
Romelu Lukaku (ST) — Has been dealing with a hamstring injury all season, last featured for Napoli on March 6. Appeared in pre-World Cup friendlies. Last international appearance: June 2026
Egypt
Mohamed Hamdi (LB) — Tore his ACL playing for Egypt at AFCON on January 5, will miss the World Cup. Last international appearance: January 2026
Mohamed Salah (RW) — Liverpool player sustained a hamstring injury on April 25. Last international appearance: January 2026
Islam Issa (LW) — Will miss the World Cup after tearing his left ACL in action against Spain on March 31. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Iran
Majid Hosseini (CB) — Has been dealing with chronic Achilles tendinitis since August, with Kayerispor confirming in February that he would miss at least six more months after surgery. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Ali Gholizadeh (RW) — Polish club Lech Poznan confirmed that the winger suffered an ACL tear in early May, will miss the World Cup. Last national team appearance: March 2026
New Zealand
Tyler Bindon (CB) — Missed Sheffield United’s last two games of the season due to an ankle injury. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Nando Pijnaker (CB) — Dislocated his right shoulder playing for Auckland FC on April 11. Last national team appearance: November 2024
Francis de Vries (LB) — Injured his right calf playing for New Zealand on March 27, expected to miss six weeks. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Group H
Spain
Fermin Lopez (CM) — Will miss the World Cup after sustaining a metatarsal fracture in his right foot that required surgery, and will rule him out until August. Occurred during Barca’s win over Betis on May 17. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Nico Williams (LW) — Suffered a hamstring injury in action for Athletic Club on May 10, but expected to be fit for the World Cup opener. Last national team appearance: September 2025
Lamine Yamal (RW) — Sustained a hamstring injury playing for Barcelona on April 22. In a race to be fit for the start of Spain’s World Cup campaign as he continues his recovery from a hamstring injury, hopeful of returning to fitness by the latter stage of the group phase. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Victor Munoz (FW) — Not part of Spain’s squad for final pre-World Cup friendly against Peru as he recovers from an unspecified injury. Luis de la Fuente says he is expected to be fit for his side’s first game of the World Cup. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Josep Lago/AFP via Getty Images
Samu Aghehowa (ST) — Set to miss the World Cup after tearing his ACL on February 9. Last national team appearance. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Cape Verde
Bruno Varela (GK) — Confirmed on April 4 that he would miss the rest of the season and the World Cup after undergoing surgery. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Jamiro Monteiro (CM) — Dutch club PEC Zwolle confirmed on February 6 that the 32-year-old would miss multiple weeks with a knee injury but would not endanger his World Cup. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Saudi Arabia
Waleed Al-Ahmed (CB) — Tore his ACL playing for Saudi Pro League club Al Qadsiyah in late March, will miss the World Cup. Last international appearance: December 2025
Uruguay
Sebastian Caceres (RB) — Sustained a facial fracture playing Mexican club America in early May, according to reports in the country. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Joaquin Piquerez (LB) — Underwent surgery on his right ankle after sustaining an injury playing for Uruguay against England on March 27. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Federico Valverde (CM) — Sustained a head injury during an altercation with Real Madrid team-mate Aurelien Tchouameni on May 7 and was diagnosed with “cranioencephalic traumatism”, will miss 10 to 14 days. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Giorgian de Arrascaeta (AM) — Underwent surgery on a fracture in his right collarbone on April 30. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Group I
France
Manu Kone (CM) — Withdrew from March’s squad after appearing to sustain a hamstring injury playing for Roma on March 19. Played in pre-tournament friendly against Ivory Coast Last national team appearance: June 2026
Hugo Ekitike (ST) — Set to miss several months after rupturing Achilles tendon. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Ousmane Dembele (ST) — Sustained discomfort in his right calf during PSG’s 2-1 Ligue 1 defeat by ⁠Paris FC on May 17, recovered to appear in Champions League final. Was an unused substitute in friendly against Ivory Coast. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Franck Fife/AFP via Getty Images
Senegal
Kalidou Koulibaly (CB) — Dealing with a quadriceps injury sustained in April. Last national team appearance: January 2026
Iraq
Youssef Amyn (LW) — AEK Larnaca winger has been absent since picking up a hamstring injury in late March, per Transfermarkt. Last national team appearance: April 2026
Ahmed Yahya (LB) — Replaced in squad by Ahmed Hassan Makenzie after suffering hamstring injury. Last national team appearance: June 2026
Norway
N/A
Group J
Argentina
Lionel Messi (FW) — Forced off in Inter Miami’s final game before the World Cup and was seen grabbing the back of his leg. Unused substitute in friendly against Honduras. Last national team appearance: April 2026
Cristian Romero (CB) — Tottenham captain will miss the rest of the domestic season with a knee injury sustained on April 12. Appeared in friendly against Honduras. Last national team appearance: June 2026
Juan Foyth (CB) — Ruptured Achilles tendon playing for Villarreal on January 24, set to miss the World Cup. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Nico Gonzalez (LW) — Atletico Madrid said that the winger sustained a thigh injury on April 28. Last national team appearance: April 2026
Joaquin Panichelli (ST) — Tore ACL in Argentina training in March, will miss the World Cup. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Algeria
Luca Zidane (GK) — Granada goalkeeper fractured his jaw and chin on April 26. Played 90 minutes and kept clean sheet against Netherlands in friendly. Last national team appearance: June 2026
Anthony Mandrea (GK) — Suffered a dislocated shoulder and not named in World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: December 2025
Melvin Mastil (GK) — Dealing with a hernia sustained in mid-April, according to L’Equipe.
Ramy Bensebaini (CB) — Borussia Dortmund centre-back will miss the remainder of the domestic season with a foot injury. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Youcef Belaili (LW) — Sustained a cruciate ligament tear in November 2025. Not in World Cup squad. Last international appearance: October 2025
Austria
Christoph Baumgartner (CM) — Ruled out of World Cup after suffering thigh injury in the pre-match warm-up before Tunisia friendly. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Jordan
Adham Al-Quraishi (RB) — Sustained an ACL tear playing for Jordan at the Arab Cup in December. Last national team appearance: October 2025
Yazan Al-Naimat (ST) — Like Al-Qureshi, tore his ACL in action for Jordan at the Arab Cup. Named in Jordan’s squad. Last national team appearance: December 2025
Ali Olwan (ST) — Sustained an undisclosed injury playing for Qatari club Al Sailiya on February 5, has not played since. Named in World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: December 2025
Group K
Portugal
Pedro Neto (LW) — Has missed both of Chelsea’s games in May due to a “knock”. Last national team appearance: April 2026
Matheus Nunes (RB) — Missed Portugal’s first pre-tournament friendly against Chile. Last national team appearance: April 2026
DR Congo
Gedeon Kalulu (CB) — Sustained a posterior cruciate ligament (PCL) tear in early February playing for Aris Limassol and is expected to miss between four and five months, according to Cypriot media. Played in friendly against Denmark. Last national team appearance: June 2026
Rocky Bushiri (CB) — Was initially named in DR Congo’s 26-man squad but was forced to withdraw later in May due to injury. Last international appearance: December 2025.
Uzbekistan
Khusniddin Alikulov (CB) — Will miss the World Cup after suffering an ACL tear on January 19, according to local media. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Jaloliddin Masharipov (LW) — Iranian club Esteghal confirmed on January 31 that the 32-year-old winger underwent surgery abroad. Last national team appearance: June 2025
Colombia
Juan Cabal (LB) — Has been out for Juventus since February and not named in squad. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Deiver Machado (LB) — Sustained a hamstring injury playing for French team Nantes in early May, missed final two games of the season. Returned for pre-World Cup friendly against Costa Rica. Last national team appearance: June 2026
Yaser Asprilla (RW) — Has not featured since sustaining a knee injury in action for Galatasaray on April 8. Not in 26-man squad. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Group L
England
Noni Madueke (RW) — Forced off during Arsenal’s final game of the season and was seen holding the back of his left leg. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Jarrad Branthwaite (CB) — Suffered a hamstring injury playing for Everton on April 19. Not named in final squad. Last national team appearance: June 2024
Tino Livramento (RB) — Dealing with a thigh injury and missed the end of the season but was included in World Cup squad and appeared in friendly against New Zealand. Last national team appearance: June 2026
Ben White (RB) — Suffered a suspected right MCL knee injury playing for Arsenal against West Ham on May 10, missed Arsenal’s final three games of 2025-26 season and not in 26-man squad. Last national team appearance: March 2026
Croatia
Luka Modric (CM) — AC Milan confirmed that the 40-year-old had undergone surgery for a fractured cheekbone on April 27, will miss the rest of the season but is expected to return for the World Cup. Played in two pre-tournament friendlies. Last national team appearance: June 2026
Stefano Rellandini/AFP via Getty Images
Ghana
Mohammed Salisu (CB) — Suffered a torn ACL playing for Monaco against Lyon on January 3, expected to miss the World Cup. Last national team appearance: October 2025
Tariq Lamptey (RB) — Tore ACL in action for Fiorentina on September 21. Not named in squad. Last national team appearance: October 2024
Abu Francis (CM) — Sustained a double leg fracture playing for Ghana against Japan on November 14. Not named in squad. Last national team appearance: November 2025
Mohammed Kudus (RW) — Has not played since suffering a quad injury in action for Tottenham on January 4. Not named in squad. Last national team appearance: October 2025
Panama
Carlos Harvey (DM) Minnesota United midfielder is out for multiple weeks due to a lower-body injury sustained in training in April, according to local media. Played in pre-tournament friendlies. Last national team appearance: June 2026
Edward Cedeno (DM) — Albacete midfielder underwent surgery on a hamstring injury in late January. Not in World Cup squad. Last national team appearance: October 2025
Sam Joseph is a Staff Editor at The Athletic, based in London. Prior to joining, he worked as an Associate Producer for CNN Sport and attended Cardiff University.
THE ATHLETIC
Never miss a single moment.
Check our tournament hub for on-the-ground reporting, instant scores, and expert analysis as the drama unfolds.
Discover more
Jun 26, 2026

Spot the pattern. Connect the terms
Find the hidden link between sports terms
_note: 全文走 summary 块A(LLM三判后只增不改);processed=成熟仓库累积,raw=本次收集过程。
